title: api: typed filter structs (Option<ChatKind> not Option<&str>)
description:
chat_query takes filter_kind: Option<&str> — a stringly-typed filter for an enum-valued field. Adding more filter fields as &str will compound the problem. Callers must know the magic strings and cannot rely on the compiler to catch typos.

Design: define filter structs using proper enum types:

  #[derive(Default)]
  pub struct ChatFilter {
      pub kind: Option<ChatKind>,
      pub muted: Option<bool>,
      pub space_id: Option<Id>,
  }

  #[derive(Default)]
  pub struct ContactFilter {
      pub presence: Option<ContactPresence>,
      pub search_string: Option<String>,
      pub in_chat_id: Option<Id>,
  }

Define filter structs for: Chat, Message, ChatContact, Space, CustomEmoji.

Each filter struct serializes to the correct JMAP filter JSON (serde rename_all = camelCase).

Tests: assert that ChatFilter { kind: Some(ChatKind::Channel), ..Default::default() } serializes to {"kind": "channel"}.
